name: fennel old bay soup
notes:
A clean italian soup. Soffrito (yellow onion, celery, carrot) + fresh tomato as the base, white beans or lentils as the protein, fennel powder and old bay for flavor and complexity.

HERBS: all fine herbs (parsley, tarragon) clash here — they fight the fennel/old bay axis and turn the soup muddled. Robust herbs (rosemary, thyme) are OK as secondary additions to diversify, but unnecessary — fennel powder and old bay already do most of the flavor work.

GREENS: mixed greens and spinach (flavorless on their own) are excellent additions because they bring bitterness, nutrition, and most importantly color, without competing with the established flavors.

GLUTAMATE INJECTIONS ARE ANTIPATTERNS: tomato paste, nutritional yeast, soy sauce, etc. all distract from the fennel + old bay backbone. Porcini mushroom powder is the one exception — it leans italian and adds quiet depth — but use sparingly.

NO STOCK / BOUILLON: stock or bouillon is also an antipattern here. It defeats the structural point of this soup. If you want an injected-stock soup, make something french instead — this is a soffrito-and-tomato soup that builds its own body.

BEANS: white beans are superior to lentils, but both work. Both are better than chickpeas, which are too harsh and break the dish.
